Wednesday, May 27, 2026

Wednesday, May 27, 2026 · 9 species· 33 detections ★ 3 life-list first

The mountain chickadee is a small songbird, a passerine bird in the tit family Paridae.

Happy Wednesday. This morning’s most memorable surprise was an honest-to-goodness life bird: a Fox Sparrow. That’s the kind of thing that can tilt the whole listen in a new direction, even on a short session. A bird you’ve never had before always seems to sharpen the edges of everything else.

The opening stretch had a nice mountain-woods feel to it. Olive-sided Flycatcher was right there from the start, which always adds a little drama to the soundscape, and Steller’s Jay gave the morning some attitude. A little later, MacGillivray’s Warbler kept things lively, and Black-headed Grosbeak rounded it out with a welcome bit of richness. It felt like a compact, busy slice of late spring.

The other fun wrinkle was that the Fox Sparrow wasn’t the only life-list addition. Mountain Chickadee and Purple Finch also landed as firsts, which is a pretty delightful way for an ordinary Wednesday to overperform. Even allowing for a bit of caution with any automated listening, those are the kinds of names that make you go back over the morning with extra interest.

Overall, this was one of those sessions that felt more textured than large: not about sheer variety for its own sake, but about a few voices giving the morning real shape. Warbling Vireo and the jays helped fill in the background, and the whole thing added up to a satisfying listen.

Nine species isn’t a bad way to start the day, especially when three of them nudge the life list forward. I’ll take that kind of Wednesday anytime.

Today's cast

MacGillivray's Warbler
Geothlypis tolmiei
×8 · 93% best confidence · 06:02–06:46 ✓ local

MacGillivray's warbler is a species of New World warbler. These birds are sluggish and heavy warblers, preferring to spend most of their time on, or near the ground, except when singing.

Olive-sided Flycatcher
Contopus cooperi
×7 · 96% best confidence · 06:00–06:06 ✓ local

The olive-sided flycatcher is a small to medium sized passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatcher family.

Steller's Jay
Cyanocitta stelleri
×7 · 96% best confidence · 06:00–06:39 ✓ local

Steller's jay is a bird native to western North America and the mountains of Central America, closely related to the blue jay found in eastern North America. It is the only crested jay west of the Rocky Mountains.

Black-headed Grosbeak
Pheucticus melanocephalus
×5 · 85% best confidence · 06:36–06:46 ✓ local

The black-headed grosbeak is a medium-sized, seed-eating bird in the family Cardinalidae. It is sometimes considered conspecific with the rose-breasted grosbeak with which it hybridizes on the American Great Plains.

Warbling Vireo
Vireo gilvus
×2 · 77% best confidence · 06:05–06:48

The warbling vireo is a small North American songbird.

California Scrub-Jay
Aphelocoma californica
×1 · 72% best confidence · 06:07 ✓ local

The California scrub jay is a species of scrub jay native to western North America. It ranges from southern British Columbia throughout California and western Nevada near Reno to west of the Sierra Nevada.

Fox Sparrow
Passerella iliaca
×1 · 69% best confidence · 06:39 ★ first

The fox sparrow is a large New World sparrow. It is the only member of the genus Passerella, although some authors split the species into four.

Mountain Chickadee
Poecile gambeli
×1 · 69% best confidence · 06:44 ★ first

The mountain chickadee is a small songbird, a passerine bird in the tit family Paridae.

Purple Finch
Haemorhous purpureus
×1 · 52% best confidence · 06:34 ★ first ✓ local

The purple finch is a bird in the finch family, Fringillidae. It breeds in the northern United States, southern Canada, and the west coast of North America.
